title: ficus johannis subsp. afghanistanica
intro: Ficus johannis subsp. afghanistanica, also known as the Afghan ficus, is a subspecies of the Ficus johannis plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan. The leaves and bark of the Afghan ficus are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ficus johannis subsp. afghanistanica include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. Ficus johannis subsp. afghanistanica is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which are used to treat various health conditions.
